missingness.irregular_time: Frontier sources were sampled at publication and announcement time; downstream benchmark revisions may change evaluation meaning.
missingness.impact_on_claim: The gate can organize evidence routes and block unsupported promotion, but it cannot prove biomedical or veterinary validity without external curator review and task-level replication.

revision_reason: Prior discovery gates captured mechanism, privacy, perturbation, and counterfactual structure, but did not require the evidence route to remain portable across models, tools, species, modalities, sites, and time.
falsification_route: Compare curator reconstruction accuracy for ungated claim packets versus route-portable packets; revise or remove this gate if it does not improve reconstruction, reveal boundary failures, or reduce unsupported promotion.
next_executable_action: Run the validator test suite and then integrate this packet as a pre-promotion check in the discovery-memory pipeline.

claim.text: Discovery-memory packets should expose portable evidence routes before being promoted as reusable scientific or biomedical infrastructure knowledge.
claim.allowed_scope: Research organization, schema design, benchmark design, source-map construction, and prototype requirement planning.
claim.disallowed_scope: Personal medical advice, veterinary diagnosis, cure claims, treatment selection, or claims that one benchmark result transfers to all domains.

evidence_route.input: Public frontier sources on scientific AI, biology agents, longitudinal health benchmarks, animal-health AI infrastructure, and privacy-preserving memory.
evidence_route.transformation: Extract recurring evaluation pressure: realistic tasks require reconstructable workflows rather than polished final answers.
evidence_route.output: Requirement that every promoted claim preserve its evidence path and decision boundary.
evidence_route.portability_risk: Search snippets and announcements may omit benchmark limitations or later revisions.
evidence_route.reconstruction_requirement: Store source status, publication date or release date, and the exact role each source played in the design implication.

evidence_route.input: Extracted design implication plus prior MC gate architecture.
evidence_route.transformation: Convert the implication into required packet fields and validation invariants.
evidence_route.output: Evidence Route Portability Gate schema and validator.
evidence_route.portability_risk: A schema can become ceremonial if tests do not include realistic failure modes.
evidence_route.reconstruction_requirement: Include valid and invalid synthetic fixtures that show how unsupported, private, or weak-evidence packets are blocked.

evidence_route.input: Synthetic gate packet describing the evidence route and transfer boundaries.
evidence_route.transformation: Run validator and regression test expectations.
evidence_route.output: Pass or fail signal for discovery-memory promotion readiness.
evidence_route.portability_risk: Validator checks structural readiness, not scientific truth.
evidence_route.reconstruction_requirement: Pair validator success with curator review and external replication before treating a claim as strong evidence.

transfer_boundaries.species: Human-health findings cannot be silently transferred to animal-health contexts; species boundary must be named and justified.
transfer_boundaries.modality: Text, EHR tables, imaging, omics, wearables, and behavior logs require separate provenance and missingness declarations.
transfer_boundaries.model: A claim generated by one model or agent must remain reconstructable by another model without hidden context.
transfer_boundaries.site: Single-site datasets and clinical or veterinary institutions may encode local workflow artifacts.
transfer_boundaries.time: Evidence cutoffs and benchmark versions must be preserved because frontier systems and datasets change quickly.
transfer_boundaries.privacy: Public literature routes and private memory routes must be separated; sensitive details require local-only or blocked promotion status.
